case_title: GOLDEN GATE EDITIONS LLC et al VS. PETER MARS et al

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETER MARS' MOTION TO DISMISS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO STAY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. CALIFORNIA IS NOT A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM AND NEITHER THE PUBLIC NOR THE PRIVATE FACTORS SUPPORT A DISMISSAL OR A STAY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS GOLDEN GATE EDITIONS LLC. AND LORI DODGE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THERE IS NO PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT AS DEFENDANT IS GRANTED LEAVE TO REQUEST A TRIAL CONTINUANCE WITH DISCOVERY AND SUMMARY MOTION DATES TRIGGERED OFF THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETER MARS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE EXCUSE FROM NON-PERFORMANCE AT 19, THIS ALLEGATION IS NEITHER PLED NOR INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE IN THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, AND PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD THIS ALLEGATION. FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, RESCISSION IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION, BUT A REMEDY, AND PLAINTIFFS MAY SEEK THAT REMEDY UNDER OTHER CLAIMS. PLAINTIFFS HAVE ADEQUATELY PLED FACTS TO ALLEGE THEIR ABUSE OF PROCESS, FRAUD AND DECLARATORY RELIEF CLAIMS. (COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER MIRRORING THE COURT'S RULING AND EMAIL TO COURT- COPYING OPPOSING COUNSEL- FOR SIGNATURE). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SANDRA SCOTT, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF SANDRA SCOTT, 415.362.9975, FOR PLAINTIFF GOLDEN GATE EDITIONS LLC.
NICHOLAS LIND, ESQ., AND C. SCOTT GREENE, ESQ., BRYAN CAVE, 415.675.3467 AND 415.675.3452, FOR DEFENDANT PETER MARS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT PETER MARS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the first cause of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action, and overruled as to the second, third and fifth causes of action. For the first cause of action, although Plaintiffs allege excuse from non-performance at 19, this allegation is neither pled nor incorporated by reference in the first cause of action, and Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to add this allegation. For the fourth cause of action, rescission is not a cause of action, but a remedy, and plaintiffs may seek that remedy under other claims. Plaintiffs have adequately pled facts to allege their abuse of process, fraud and declaratory relief claims. (counsel for plaintiff to prepare a form of order mirroring the court's ruling and email to court- copying opposing counsel- for signature). Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Off Calendar Dec-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PETER MARS' MOTION TO DISMISS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO STAY.
Appearances: Sandra Scott of the Law Office of Sandra Scott (362.9975) for plaintiff and responding party; Stephanie A. Blazewicz of Bryan Cave (675.3418) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. California is not a seriously inconvenient forum and neither the public nor the private factors support a dismissal or a stay. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288
